feed.title: Scholar Alerts — Clean Feed
feed.subtitle: Google Scholar alerts reformatted as individual article entries

entry.title: The AI Skills Shift: Mapping Skill Obsolescence, Emergence , and Transition Pathways in the LLM Era
entry.content:
Authors: R Jadhav, J Danve -arXivpreprintarXiv:2604.06906, 2026

Abstract: As Large Language Models reshape the global labor market, policymakers and workers need empirical data on which occupational skills may be most susceptible to automation. We present the Skill Automation Feasibility Index (SAFI) ...

Alert: arxiv" + intitle:"emergence (keyword alert)

entry.title: Emergence of Non-Markovian Classical-Quantum Dynamics from Decoherence
entry.content:
Authors: S Tomizuka, H Takeda -arXivpreprintarXiv:2604.06891, 2026

Abstract: The quantum nature of gravity remains experimentally unverified, despite recent proposals to probe it using tabletop experiments such as gravity-mediated entanglement schemes. In parallel, consistent formulations of classical--quantum ...

Alert: arxiv" + intitle:"emergence (keyword alert)

entry.title: Dissipative Hamilton Jacobi Dynamics and the Emergence of Quantum Wave Mechanics
entry.content:
Authors: NJ Matjelo -arXivpreprintarXiv:2604.06455, 2026

Abstract: We develop a dissipative extension of classical mechanics based on a complex, and more generally quaternionic, action principle that endows every classical system with an intrinsic environment. Decomposing the action into conservative and ...

Alert: arxiv" + intitle:"emergence (keyword alert)

entry.title: Emergence of cooperation in nonlinear higher-order public goods games
entry.content:
Authors: J Llabrés, O Sadekar, F Malizia, F Battiston -arXivpreprintarXiv:2604.07228, 2026

Abstract: ... Evolutionary game theory has provided substantial contributions to explain the emergence of cooperation under unfavourable conditions in ... Here, we study the emergence of cooperation in a nonlinear public goods game (PGG) on hypergraphs ...

Alert: arxiv" + intitle:"emergence (keyword alert)

entry.title: Tracking the emergence of linguistic structure in self-supervised models learning from speech
entry.content:
Authors: M de Heer Kloots, M Bentum, H Mohebbi, C Pouw... -arXive-prints, 2026

Abstract: Self-supervised speech models learn effective representations of spoken language, which have been shown to reflect various aspects of linguistic structure. But when does such structure emerge in model training? We study the encoding of a wide ...

Alert: arxiv" + intitle:"emergence (keyword alert)
